_annotation_meta.source_quality: Poster PDF + press release (full text extracted)

paper.title: Targeted Lipid Nanoparticle Delivery in Non-Human Primates Enables In Vivo HBG1/2 Promoter Editing for β-hemoglobinopathies
paper.authors: A. Gupta, J. Raghav, E. Kaye, L. Li, S. Sherman, M. Thompson, P. Zhang, R. Viswanathan, S. Iovino, E. Marco, G. Giannoukos, T.M. Teslovich, L. Burkly, J. Zuris, S. Bowley

formulations.helper_lipid: PROPRIETARY — poster mentions 'helper lipid' in schematic but no name
formulations.peg_lipid: PROPRIETARY — 'lipid-anchored PEG' shown in schematic, type/length undisclosed
formulations.targeting_details: Proprietary targeting moiety — called 'moiety 2' (LNP2) in NHP data. Exact identity (antibody, peptide, aptamer?) not disclosed. Conjugated to PEG anchor. Poster states 'highly specific and scalable conjugation chemistry to link targeting moiety to PEG'. Earlier optimization used a different moiety (switch from moiety 1 to moiety 2 shown in Fig 3).
formulations.formulation_notes: CRITICAL GAP: All formulation details are proprietary. No lipid names, molar ratios, size, PDI, or zeta potential disclosed. The tLNP schematic (Fig 1) shows 5 components: ionizable lipid, helper lipid, cholesterol, lipid-anchored PEG, and targeting moiety — standard 4-component LNP + targeting. Patent filings noted. Key design principles stated: PEG + helper lipid composition/ratio optimized for liver de-targeting; targeting moiety promotes HSC uptake via receptor; iterative optimization over multiple design cycles.

experiments.dose_other_units: Single IV dose (exact mg/kg not stated for GFP group; editing group was 2 mg/kg — likely same)
experiments.strain_or_model: Female NHPs (species not specified in poster — likely cynomolgus based on standard Editas practices)

results.liver_notes: QUALITATIVE ONLY — ISH and IHC show 'distribution limited to sinusoidal spaces and sinusoidal cells' for tLNP vs 'high delivery in hepatocytes' for standard LNP (Fig 6). Described as 'minimal in hepatocytes'. No quantitative % reported. This is a CRITICAL gap — we cannot place Editas on the Pareto without a liver number.

safety.tolerability_notes: Ovary: negative in developing oocytes (ISH/IHC). No detectable Cas12a protein in Day 30 bone marrow samples.

experiments.figure_source: Fig 4A (HSC delivery), Fig 6 (liver biodistribution ISH/IHC images)
experiments.data_confidence: medium (HSC ~75% stated in text but approximate; liver is qualitative only)
experiments.notes: GFP group (Group 2): 3 female NHPs, sacrificed 24h post-dose for biodistribution. The ~75% GFP+ HSCs represents delivery/uptake, not editing.

experiments.payload: AsCas12a mRNA + HBG1/2 crRNA (proprietary engineered Cas12a)
experiments.strain_or_model: Female NHPs (species not specified, likely cynomolgus)

results.hsc_range_or_sd: Up to ~58% (mean stated as 58% in press release; poster shows individual animal trajectories in Fig 4B but values not individually digitizable from poster text extraction)
results.liver_notes: Not assessed in editing group (only GFP group had liver ISH/IHC)

functional_readout.hbf_induction: Not measured (NHP HBG1/2 editing doesn't directly translate to HbF measurement in NHP globin context)

safety.tolerability_notes: Poster states 'favorable biodistribution profile' but no quantitative safety data shown for editing group. PK data shows prolonged plasma circulation vs standard liver LNP (Fig 5).

experiments.figure_source: Fig 4B (editing time course), Fig 5 (PK), press release
experiments.data_confidence: high (58% mean explicitly stated in press release text; 2 mg/kg dose confirmed)
experiments.notes: CRITICAL DATA POINT. Editing time course from Fig 4B: ~17% at 1 week, ~29% at 1 week (different animal?), ~36% at 2 weeks, ~21% at 2 weeks (different animal?), then editing climbs to 58% mean at 5 months. Poster states 'clonal profile is consistent across all timepoints' suggesting durable HSC editing. Therapeutic threshold set at ≥25% (based on Fitzhugh 2017 allogeneic transplant data). The ~48% editing noted in humanized mouse model (Fig 3) preceded NHP optimization.

experiments.strain_or_model: NBSGW (NOD.Cg-PrkdcscidIl2rgtm1Wjl/SzJ x C57BL/6J-KitW-41J/J) engrafted with human CD34+ cells from mobilized peripheral blood, without irradiation
experiments.figure_source: Fig 3B (~48% HSC editing), Fig 3A (HSPC editing time course)
experiments.data_confidence: medium (stated as '~48%' in poster, read from figure labels)
experiments.notes: Iterative optimization history shown in Fig 3: started at ~0.25% (early formulation), progressed through ~17%, ~29%, ~36%, ~21% over moiety switches and optimization cycles, culminating in ~48% HSC editing and ~36% HSPC editing at 2 weeks. The ~48% was achieved in humanized mouse before NHP translation.

key_findings_for_project: HIGHEST HSC EDITING IN NHP TO DATE: 58% mean HBG1/2 editing in HSCs at 5 months, single 2 mg/kg IV dose, no mobilization/conditioning — sets a new benchmark for the field.
Liver de-targeting is qualitative only (ISH/IHC images show 'minimal in hepatocytes' vs standard LNP) — CANNOT be added to Pareto without quantitative liver %.
Prolonged plasma circulation of tLNP vs standard liver LNP suggests PEG/helper optimization for reduced hepatocyte uptake.
AsCas12a editing (not Cas9) — different nuclease may have different editing kinetics/persistence profile.
Formulation is ENTIRELY proprietary — zero overlap with our feature matrix. Cannot be used as a training data point for ML models.
Therapeutic threshold of ≥25% HSC editing (from allogeneic transplant literature) is convincingly exceeded, validating in vivo approach.

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Editas uses proprietary targeting (unknown receptor) vs Breda's anti-CD117. Editas achieves 58% editing vs Breda's Cre-lox recombination (not directly comparable metrics). Both use NBSGW humanized mice. Editas has NHP data; Breda does not.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Different delivery class entirely — Shi uses untargeted LNPs. Not directly comparable.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Kim (Nature Biotech 2024) uses untargeted LNP67 with 2 mg/kg in NHP achieving GFP delivery to CD34+ cells. Editas achieves 58% editing (harder metric) at same dose. Both lack quantitative liver numbers for NHP studies.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: CANNOT be added to Pareto — liver biodistribution is qualitative only (ISH/IHC images, no % value). If Editas ever publishes quantitative liver %, and it's low (say <5%), this would be the single best Pareto point in existence (58% HSC editing with near-zero liver).
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Does NOT directly update EC30/EC50 because this is NHP editing data (different species, AsCas12a). However, 58% editing at 2 mg/kg in NHP dramatically strengthens the case that therapeutic editing is achievable in vivo. May inform a 'NHP benchmark' row in Table 1.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Cannot validate or invalidate gap formulations because Editas formulation is entirely proprietary. However, the design principles (PEG/helper for liver de-targeting, targeting moiety on PEG) are consistent with our gap formulation hypotheses.

limitations_and_caveats: ALL formulation details proprietary — cannot create a dataset row with lipid composition features. ZERO overlap with ML feature matrix.
Liver biodistribution is QUALITATIVE ONLY (ISH/IHC images) — the single most important missing number for our project.
NHP species not explicitly stated (likely cynomolgus but not confirmed).
Only 3 animals per group — small sample size.
HBG1/2 editing measured by indel %, but HbF protein levels not measured in NHP.
Exact dose for GFP group not confirmed (assumed 2 mg/kg like editing group).
No off-target editing data reported.
Individual animal data not extractable from poster — only means and approximate values.
Poster is from EHA June 2025 — may have updated data since then (e.g., 'ongoing evaluation of further optimized formulations' noted).

_dataset_summary.new_rows: 0 rows for ML feature matrix (formulation is proprietary). 2-3 rows for therapeutic window comparison table (NHP editing context only — E1 delivery, E2 editing, E3 mouse editing).
_dataset_summary.therapeutic_window_change: Adds NHP 58% editing benchmark at 2 mg/kg. Does NOT change EC30/EC50 estimates (different species/nuclease). Strengthens narrative that in vivo HSC editing above therapeutic threshold is achievable.

_annotation_meta.source_quality: ASH abstract only (no poster/full paper). Abstract #1067, Blood 2025;146(Suppl 1):1067. Access via search snippet — full text behind paywall/403.

paper.title: Scalable, Antibody-Free Lipid Nanoparticle Achieves Human HSPC-Selective mRNA Delivery In Vivo Without Conditioning
paper.authors: Kim H et al. (Dahlman lab, Georgia Tech)

formulations.helper_lipid: Known from Kim 2024 Nature Biotech — likely DOPE or variant (refer to existing dataset rows)
formulations.peg_lipid: Known from Kim 2024 — refer to existing dataset (C14-PEG2000 likely)
formulations.targeting_details: Antibody-free — key differentiator. Abstract explicitly emphasizes scalability and freeze-thaw stability vs antibody-conjugated LNPs.
formulations.formulation_notes: This is the same LNP67 platform from Kim 2024 Nature Biotech. Exact composition should match your existing 110-row dataset entries for LNP67. The ASH 2025 abstract adds humanized mouse functional data but no new formulation details. Abstract states 'manufacturing complexity and instability following freeze-thaw cycles' as disadvantages of antibody-conjugated LNPs that LNP67 avoids.

experiments.payload: aVHH mRNA (anti-VHH nanobody reporter — detectable by flow cytometry)
experiments.strain_or_model: NBSGW humanized mice engrafted with human CD34+ cells (likely same model as Kim 2024). Multiple human donors tested including one older donor (age 74).

results.hsc_range_or_sd: Median 37%, range 22-65% (from abstract text: values explicitly stated)
results.liver_notes: NOT REPORTED — CRITICAL FLAG. Abstract mentions 'immunofluorescence analysis of non-hematopoietic tissues, including liver, spleen, and lung, showed strong co-localization of hCD45 and aVHH with minimal signal in mCD45+ cells'. This means delivery to human hematopoietic cells in those organs, NOT to parenchymal cells. Liver PARENCHYMAL delivery is NOT quantified. This is consistent with Kim 2024 where liver delivery to hepatocytes was substantial but not well-separated from hematopoietic cells in liver.

experiments.data_confidence: high (explicit numbers with ranges stated in abstract text)
experiments.notes: KEY DATA POINTS from abstract: CMP 70.45% (51.4-84.6%), GMP 59.55% (37.5-84.3%), MEP 52.15% (40.2-80.0%). Mature cells much lower: myeloid 29.04% (14.4-52.4%), NK 11.4% (2.01-26.6%), B cells 5.1% (2.23-9.29%), T cells 3.7% (0.39-7.52%). Erythroid (CD71+GlyA+, CD71-GlyA+) and megakaryocytes (CD41+) <5%. This shows strong HSPC selectivity with decreasing delivery down the differentiation hierarchy. One older donor (age 74) showed lower overall efficiency but similar lineage patterns. This is aVHH DELIVERY, not editing — but validates dose-response at 2 mg/kg for human HSPCs.

key_findings_for_project: First human HSPC validation for LNP67 in humanized mice at 2 mg/kg: 37% median HSC delivery (range 22-65%).
Beautiful differentiation hierarchy: HSC 37% > CMP 70% > GMP 60% > MEP 52% >> myeloid 29% >> NK 11% > B 5% > T 4% — confirms HSPC selectivity.
Antibody-free platform with freeze-thaw stability — manufacturing advantage for clinical translation.
Liver delivery NOT QUANTIFIED — cannot add to Pareto.
Age-dependent delivery efficiency noted (74yo donor lower) — potential stratification variable for clinical trials.
Validates LNP67 in human HSPCs in vivo, not just mouse/NHP models.

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: LNP67 is antibody-free vs Breda's anti-CD117 conjugation. 37% HSC delivery (aVHH) vs Breda's ~80-90% delivery (Cre recombination in reporter mice). Different metrics and models make direct comparison difficult, but lower absolute delivery is expected without targeting.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Shi 2023 used untargeted LNPs in wild-type mice. Kim 2025 ASH now validates the humanized mouse model — closer to clinical relevance.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: DIRECT UPDATE to Kim 2024 Nature Biotech. The 2024 paper showed NHP CD34+ delivery at 0.25-0.4 mg/kg. This ASH abstract shows humanized mouse delivery at 2 mg/kg with detailed progenitor subtype breakdown. This is the missing human HSPC layer.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: CANNOT add to Pareto — liver delivery not quantified.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Updates dose-response: at 2 mg/kg, median 37% HSC delivery. Combined with Kim 2024 NHP data (lower delivery at 0.25-0.4 mg/kg), this sketches a dose-response curve for LNP67. May update EC30/EC50 for delivery (not editing) in humanized mouse context.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: LNP67 composition is already in dataset. This adds a new efficacy data point at 2 mg/kg in humanized mice. Validates gap formulation G1 (if any gap formulation predicts similar HSPC selectivity pattern).

limitations_and_caveats: Abstract only — no poster or full paper figures to digitize.
Liver parenchymal delivery NOT quantified — the IF analysis only shows hCD45+/aVHH+ co-localization, not hepatocyte delivery.
HSC delivery is aVHH protein expression, not editing — likely overestimates functional editing capacity.
PPZ-A10 SMILES now known (CAS 2941268-67-1, confirmed via Ni et al. 2022 + US Patent 2025/0127727). Bridges to external datasets.
N animals per group not stated.
Timepoint not stated in abstract (likely 24-48h based on Kim 2024 protocol).
No dose-response within this study (single 2 mg/kg dose).

_dataset_summary.new_rows: 1 major new row: LNP67 at 2 mg/kg in humanized mouse with detailed subtype breakdown (HSC + CMP + GMP + MEP + mature cells). Could be expanded to ~6 rows if each progenitor subtype is a separate record. Formulation features already in dataset from Kim 2024.
_dataset_summary.therapeutic_window_change: Adds humanized mouse delivery data point to LNP67 dose-response. Median 37% HSC delivery at 2 mg/kg. Does not change editing-based EC30/EC50 (this is delivery, not editing).

_annotation_meta.source_quality: Full peer-reviewed paper (Nature Biotechnology, open access). Text extracted from HTML — figures not directly digitizable but key numbers stated in abstract and results text.

paper.title: In vivo gene editing of human hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells using envelope-engineered virus-like particles
paper.authors: Botchkarev VV Jr, Harrington S, Stoppato M, Justen A, Kimber C, Kapuria A, Gibson KM, Chu CS, Xu Y, Haugh K, Ankala R, Kipniss N, DeGroot A, Moore E, de Jesus R, Adewale F, Daniels K, Crocker S, Liang A, Joyce S, Roberto N, Angel P, Smith D, Wong A, Adler N, Berlfein V, Amatya S, Cruite P, Usmani S, Ruzo A, Ferland B, Chandra S, Rebar EJ, Shah JV, Trudeau K, Biasco L

formulations.targeting_details: Optimized BaEVTR (baboon endogenous viral envelope glycoprotein, truncated R peptide). Modified with engineered protease site for more efficient R peptide removal + VLA-4/5 integrin binding moiety. Pseudotyped on MLV capsid with Cas9-gag fusion. Binds ASCT1/2 solute carriers (broadly expressed).
formulations.formulation_notes: VLP platform — fundamentally different from LNP. MLV capsid with gag-Cas9 RNP fusion. Delivers pre-formed ribonucleoprotein (not mRNA). This is important: editing is independent of target cell transcriptional state, critical for quiescent LT-HSPCs. No viral genome = integrating-free. BaEVTR has complement resistance and low pre-existing immunity in humans.

formulations.targeting_details: Nipah virus (NiV) fusion complex re-engineered with custom CD133-binding domain. Designed to reduce transduction of mature blood cells and increase HSPC specificity vs BaEVTR. NiV envelope is modular, allowing receptor retargeting.
formulations.formulation_notes: CD133-targeted variant achieves higher HSPC specificity in vivo vs BaEVTR. CD133 (prominin-1) is expressed on primitive HSPCs. Key advantage: reduced off-target transduction of mature blood cells.

experiments.dose_other_units: MOI-based (VLP dosing, not mg/kg — exact MOI in vivo not stated in abstract)
experiments.strain_or_model: Humanized mouse model (specific strain not extracted from abstract — likely NBSGW or NSG engrafted with human CD34+ cells)

results.hsc_definition: Long-term HSPCs (LT-HSPCs — specific markers not in abstract, likely Lin-CD34+CD38-CD90+CD45RA-)
results.liver_notes: Abstract states 'both VLPs avoid human hepatocytes in a humanized liver mouse model' and text states 'both VLPs avoid human hepatocytes upon systemic administration in vivo in a humanized liver mouse model'. Ensoma paper intro also notes LNPs 'retain substantial biodistribution in off-target filter organs such as the spleen and the liver' — positioning VLPs as liver-avoiding. NEAR-ZERO LIVER — this is a PARETO IDEAL feature.

experiments.notes: 31% B2M editing in LT-HSPCs at 8 weeks post-dosing. This is DURABLE editing in the most primitive compartment. Combined with near-zero liver, this sits in the Pareto ideal zone.
26% BCL11A editing at 5 days. Early timepoint — would expect this to be sustained given LT-HSPC targeting. BCL11A is the validated therapeutic target (same as Casgevy).

functional_readout.hbf_induction: Yes — HBG1/2 editing induces HbF (same target as Editas)

experiments.notes: 7.5% HBG1/2 editing at 5 days is lower than BCL11A (26%) — likely reflects different gRNA efficiency or target accessibility. For comparison, Editas achieved ~48% HBG1/2 in humanized mouse and 58% in NHP using tLNP. The VLP achieves this with near-zero liver but lower absolute editing.

experiments.strain_or_model: Primary human HSPCs, resting (no cytokine stimulation)
experiments.notes: In vitro on resting HSPCs: 75% B2M- by FC (93% by HTS) for bulk HSPCs. HSC subtype: 38% at MOI 16, 61% at MOI 83 by HTS. Flow cytometry underestimates vs sequencing. Resting conditions critical — mimics in vivo quiescent HSC state.

key_findings_for_project: CROSS-PLATFORM COMPARATOR: VLP achieves 31% LT-HSPC editing at 8 weeks with near-zero liver — sits in Pareto ideal zone.
Near-zero liver hepatocyte delivery confirmed in humanized liver model — major advantage over LNPs.
Two envelope designs: BaEVTR (broad HSPC) and CD133-targeted NiV (higher HSPC specificity) — CD133 variant reduces mature cell transduction.
RNP delivery (not mRNA) means editing is transcription-independent — advantage for quiescent LT-HSCs.
26% BCL11A editing at 5 days — clinically relevant target, same as Casgevy.
7.5% HBG1/2 at 5 days — lower than LNP competitors but with clean liver profile.
Ensoma explicitly positions VLPs as superior to LNPs for liver avoidance, citing Breda 2023 and Kim 2024.

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Breda uses CD117-targeted LNP (mRNA delivery); Ensoma uses BaEVTR/CD133 VLP (RNP delivery). Breda has ~80-90% delivery in reporter mice but significant liver off-target. Ensoma has 31% editing with near-zero liver. Different metrics complicate comparison but Ensoma wins on selectivity.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Shi uses untargeted LNPs — liver delivery is substantial. Ensoma VLPs avoid liver entirely. Not directly comparable platforms.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Kim LNP67 has substantial liver delivery (not quantified in ASH abstract but implied). Ensoma near-zero liver. For same ~30% HSC range, Ensoma has dramatically better selectivity. However, Ensoma requires mobilization for HDAd platform (NHP poster) while LNP67 does not.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: YES — 31% B2M editing in LT-HSPCs + near-zero liver CAN be added to Pareto IF we code liver as ~0%. This is the first clear point in the ideal zone (high BM, low liver). Caveat: VLP, not LNP, so it sits on a different 'platform curve' in the Pareto space.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Adds VLP comparator row to Table 1: 31% LT-HSPC editing (B2M), 26% BCL11A, 7.5% HBG1/2. Below Editas 58% but with much cleaner liver profile. May define a 'VLP frontier' in the therapeutic window.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Does not directly validate LNP gap formulations (different platform). However, confirms that near-zero liver + meaningful HSC editing IS achievable — validates the concept that gap formulations targeting. Suggests LNP gap formulations should aim for >31% to compete with VLPs.

limitations_and_caveats: VLP platform — cannot be directly added to LNP feature matrix. Different delivery mechanism entirely (RNP vs mRNA, envelope vs lipid bilayer).
Dose is in MOI, not mg/kg — not directly comparable to LNP dosing.
5-day timepoint for BCL11A/HBG data is very early — need longer follow-up for durability.
No dose-response data in abstract for in vivo experiments.
Quantitative liver avoidance data is in humanized liver model (not same model as HSPC editing) — different mouse strains.
Manufacturing considerations: VLPs are viral-derived particles with different production/QC requirements than LNPs.
Paper text truncated — full figures and supplementary data not extractable from HTML fetch. Would need PDF for complete digitization.
Scalability and cost of VLP manufacturing not addressed in this paper (see ASGCT poster for that).

_dataset_summary.new_rows: 3-4 new rows for cross-platform comparison table (E1: B2M 8wk, E2: BCL11A 5d, E3: HBG 5d, potentially E4 in vitro). Cannot be added to LNP feature matrix but should be added to a broader 'platform comparison' table.
_dataset_summary.therapeutic_window_change: Adds VLP comparator with 31% LT-HSPC editing + near-zero liver. Establishes that the Pareto ideal zone IS reachable. Changes narrative: LNP gap formulations need to achieve >31% HSC + <5% liver to match VLPs.

_annotation_meta.source_quality: Poster PDF (full text extracted from ASGCT 2025 poster)

paper.title: Acute safety and biodistribution profile of HSC-targeting virus-like particles based on helper-dependent adenovirus serotype 5/35++ in non-human primates
paper.authors: Patrick Au, Tiffany Vo, Robert Peters (Ensoma, Inc.)

formulations.targeting_details: HDAd5/35++ uses Ad35++ fiber knob targeting human CD46 receptor. CD46 is highly expressed on CD34+CD90+ primitive HSCs (highest gMFI vs T, B, NK, myeloid cells — Fig 1). Helper-dependent adenovirus = 'gutless' vector devoid of viral genes, 35 kB payload capacity.
formulations.formulation_notes: NOTE: This is a DIFFERENT VLP platform than the Ensoma Nature Biotech paper (BaEVTR/NiV VLP). This is an adenovirus-based helper-dependent vector (HDAd5/35++), not an MLV-based VLP. Drug product = 1:1 mix of EF1p-MGMT-EGFP therapeutic vector + Sleeping Beauty-Flp integration vector. Requires HSC mobilization (G-CSF + plerixafor) before dosing. The mobilization step is a key difference from LNPs which don't require mobilization.

experiments.dose_other_units: Group 2: 6.25E12 gc/kg (2M, 1F); Group 3: 9.25E12 gc/kg (1M, 2F); Group 1: vehicle (1F)
experiments.strain_or_model: Cynomolgus macaques (3M, 4F total), pre-screened negative for NAb/TAb
experiments.conditioning_details: G-CSF (Day -4 to -1 and Day 1) + Plerixafor (Day -1). Immune prophylaxis: Dexamethasone (Day -1 qd, Day 1 bid), Tocilizumab (Day 1 bid), Anakinra (Day 1 bid).

results.liver_notes: QUANTITATIVE BIODISTRIBUTION BY ORGAN (Day 8, qPCR): Liver shows SUBSTANTIAL VCN — appears to be among the highest organs alongside spleen and lung in the bar chart (Fig 2B). Poster states 'highest VCN detected in liver, spleen, and lung'. However, whole blood had the highest signal. Exact values not digitizable from bar chart but scale goes to 2,000,000 gc/ug DNA. KEY: The liver VCN is HIGH for HDAd — this contrasts with the Nature Biotech VLP paper which showed near-zero hepatocyte transduction. Important distinction: biodistribution (VCN) ≠ hepatocyte transduction (the Nature Biotech paper tested in a humanized liver model).

safety.cytokine_elevation: Transient: IL-6 peaked at ~2500 pg/mL at 6h, resolved by 72h. MCP-1 peaked at ~35000 pg/mL at 6h, resolved by 72h. Complement: Bb peaked at ~8 ug/mL at 6h; C3a peaked at ~250 ug/mL at 6h; CH50 showed transient decrease. All resolved by 72h.
safety.liver_toxicity: Minimal impact: AST remained <1000 U/L (transient spike in some animals); ALT remained <400 U/L; total bilirubin stable <0.5 mg/dL. CRP elevated transiently. No HDAd-related microscopic findings or gross changes on histopath.
safety.tolerability_notes: All animals survived to scheduled Day 8 terminal endpoint. No clinical observations. Platelets showed transient decrease (typical for adenoviral vectors) recovering by 72h. Myeloid hyperplasia and inflammatory infiltrates in tissues attributed to mobilization premedication, not vector. CRITICAL: No detectable HDAd biodistribution or transgene expression in gonads (ovaries and testes) by Day 8.

experiments.figure_source: Fig 2B (biodistribution bar chart), Fig 2C (transgene expression), Fig 3 (clinical chemistry panels A-K)
experiments.data_confidence: medium (bar chart values estimated; safety values read from time-course plots)
experiments.notes: TRANSGENE EXPRESSION (MGMT by RT-qPCR): Detectable in liver, spleen, lung, bone marrow, whole blood. NOT detected in gonads, frontal cortex, cerebellum, spinal cord, kidney, adrenal, lymph nodes, heart. Scale goes to 2,000,000 RNA copies. Liver and whole blood appear to have highest transgene expression.

key_findings_for_project: QUANTITATIVE NHP BIODISTRIBUTION for HDAd5/35++ VLP — liver, spleen, lung all show substantial VCN.
Safety profile: transient cytokine/complement activation resolving by 72h — expected for adenoviral vectors with immune prophylaxis.
No gonadal biodistribution — important safety signal for clinical translation.
REQUIRES MOBILIZATION (G-CSF + plerixafor) — different patient journey than LNP approaches.

Liver VCN is SUBSTANTIAL — contrast with Nature Biotech VLP paper's 'near-zero hepatocyte' claim. Key distinction: VCN in liver tissue ≠ functional hepatocyte transduction. HDAd via CD46 may be in liver immune cells, not hepatocytes.
Immune prophylaxis regimen (dex + tocilizumab + anakinra) is aggressive — clinical complexity.
Up to 9.25E12 gc/kg was well tolerated — establishes dose range for NHP.

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Completely different platform (HDAd vs LNP). Breda requires no mobilization; Ensoma HDAd requires G-CSF + plerixafor. Breda's CD117/LNP has liver off-target; Ensoma HDAd also has liver VCN.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Not comparable — different platform. But notable that both Kim LNP67 and Ensoma HDAd show liver presence.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: CANNOT add to Pareto — no HSC editing/delivery % reported in this poster (safety/biodistribution study only). The biodistribution data shows liver VCN but no HSC functional data.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: No change — no HSC efficacy data in this poster.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Not directly relevant to LNP gap formulations. However, the liver biodistribution pattern for a CD46-targeted vector is informative: even 'HSC-targeted' platforms can have substantial liver presence.

limitations_and_caveats: Safety/biodistribution only — NO HSC editing or delivery efficacy data in this poster.
Dose in gc/kg, not mg/kg — not comparable to LNP dosing.
Requires mobilization + immune prophylaxis — fundamentally different clinical approach than LNPs.
Day 8 timepoint only — no long-term safety or biodistribution data.
Bar chart values not precisely digitizable from poster text extraction.
Different VLP platform than Nature Biotech paper (HDAd5/35++ vs BaEVTR/NiV VLP on MLV capsid).
Small N: 2-3 animals per dose group.
VCN in liver tissue ≠ hepatocyte transduction — liver contains many hematopoietic cells.

_dataset_summary.new_rows: 0 rows for ML feature matrix (not an LNP, and no HSC efficacy data). Adds 1 row to NHP safety/biodistribution comparison table.

_annotation_meta.source_quality: Correction notice only (erratum eadz0744). Full text of correction confirmed via search.

paper.title: Erratum for 'In vivo hematopoietic stem cell modification by mRNA delivery'
paper.authors: Breda L, Papp TE, Triebwasser MP, et al.

key_findings_for_project: CORRECTION IS IMAGE-ONLY: The CFU assay image in Fig. 2E used for A3 (Control-IgG/LNP-Cre 1μg) was inadvertently duplicated for B1 (Untreated Ai14 BM). The B1 image has been corrected.
CRITICAL: The correction explicitly states 'This correction does not affect the interpretations of the data or the conclusions of the paper.'
NO CHANGES to any efficacy percentages, liver delivery data, dose-response data, or any quantitative values we have annotated from Breda 2023.
Our existing dataset rows from Breda 2023 remain VALID and UNCHANGED.

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: N/A — this IS the Breda correction. No data changes.

limitations_and_caveats: Figure image correction only — purely cosmetic fix to a duplicated CFU assay photo.
No quantitative data affected.
All existing annotations from Breda 2023 remain valid.

_dataset_summary.therapeutic_window_change: None. All existing Breda 2023 data remains unchanged.
